body: Hi, do you install Tesla wall connectors? We just got an EV and want a charger in the garage in Fox Chase.
Yes. Tesla and most J1772 units. Need to see panel and run distance first. Garage attached or detached?
Attached, panel is right on the other side of the garage wall.
Easy run then. Booked you Thursday morning for a look. Will text when on the way.
Looked at it Thursday, easy run off the garage panel. Quoted the Tesla install and scheduled the work. Closing this thread.
Are you licensed and insured? Need a panel upgrade and the township wants a permit.
Master electrician, fully insured. Pull permits and handle inspection myself. What amperage now and what are you going to?
100 amp now, want 200. House is from the 60s.
Standard job. Ballpark 2,800 to 3,400 depending on meter and service entrance. Come out to confirm. Set for next Tuesday.
Confirmed the 100 to 200 amp service change at the Tuesday visit. Quote sent, permit to follow. Closing out.
Do you serve Roxborough? Have knob and tube in the upstairs bedrooms and my insurance is asking about it.
Yes, cover Roxborough. Knob and tube I rewire all the time. Need to know how many rooms and if attic is accessible.
Need a couple GFCI outlets added in the kitchen and one in the bathroom. How fast can you do it?
Half day job. Can come Saturday morning. Cash or check on completion.
Saturday works, thanks.
Added the kitchen and bath GFCIs Saturday morning. Tested every outlet, paid on completion. Done.
Looking to put in recessed lighting in the living room, about 6 cans. Can you do that with a dimmer?
Six cans on a dimmer, no problem. First floor or under an attic? Changes the labor.
Second floor under the attic. Went with someone who could start sooner, sorry.
Understood. Keep my number if it falls through.
Customer went with another contractor who could start sooner. No hard feelings, closing this one.
We want a whole house generator with a transfer switch. Do you size and install those?
Install transfer switches and wire the gen. Don't supply the unit itself. What size standby are you looking at, and gas or propane?
Natural gas, probably a 22kw. Still getting quotes on the generator.
When you have the unit picked, send model and I'll spec the switch. Permit required in your township for that.
Hi, the outlets in half my kitchen stopped working. Breaker is not tripped. What would cause that?
Usually a tripped GFCI feeding the rest. Look for one with a reset button, could be behind the coffee maker. Hit reset.
That was it, found one by the sink. Works now. Thank you.
Good. If it trips again you've got a fault on the line, call me.
Resolved over the phone, tripped GFCI hiding by the sink. No visit needed. Closing.
Do you cover Bryn Mawr in Montgomery County? Need a 240v line run for a hot tub on the back patio.
Cover Montgomery County. Hot tub needs a dedicated 240v circuit with a GFCI disconnect at the tub. What's the amp draw on the unit?
Spec sheet says 50 amp. Panel is in the basement, tub is maybe 60 feet away.
Doable. 60 foot run plus disconnect, figure 950 to 1,200 with permit. Come out next week to confirm the path. Set you Wednesday.
Walked the run Wednesday, confirmed the 60 foot path and the GFCI disconnect spot at the tub. Quote stands, scheduling the install. Closing thread.
Do you go out to Doylestown in Bucks County? Old fuse box, want it replaced with a breaker panel.
Bucks is a stretch but I take service changes out there. Fuse box swap means a permit and an inspection. Send a photo of the box and the meter.
Need an EV charger installed in Media. Have a NEMA 14-50 plug already, just need it wired to the panel.
Cover Delaware County. 14-50 on a 50 amp breaker, straightforward if panel has space. Does it have two open slots?
Panel is pretty full honestly. Maybe one slot.
Then we look at a tandem or a subpanel. Don't want to overload it. Booked Saturday to assess.
Assessed Saturday, panel was too tight so going with a small subpanel for the charger. Quoted, work scheduled. Closing.
Half the lights in my house flicker when the furnace kicks on. Is that dangerous?
Flicker on a big load draw can be a loose neutral. That's not something to sit on. When did it start?
Couple weeks ago, getting worse. Can you come look?
Will check the main lugs and meter connection first. Out tomorrow morning. Don't run big appliances till I see it.
Found a loose neutral at the meter base, tightened and tested. Flicker gone, told them to have PECO recheck the lugs. Closing out.
What does a 200 amp service upgrade usually run? Selling the house and the inspector flagged the old panel.
Depends on meter location and if the utility has to be involved. Typical range is 2,800 to 3,600. What did the inspector write up exactly?
Hi, are you taking new work in Norristown? Need an outdoor outlet and some landscape lighting wired.
Taking work in Norristown. Outdoor outlet has to be GFCI and in a weatherproof box. Landscape lighting low voltage or line voltage?
Low voltage with a transformer. Sounds good, when can you start?
Next week Thursday. Bring the transformer if you've bought it, otherwise I'll spec one.
Ran the GFCI outdoor outlet and wired the low voltage transformer Thursday. Tested the landscape zones, all good. Done.
Breaker for the dryer keeps tripping. Replaced the dryer and it still does it. Bad breaker?
Could be a worn breaker or a loose connection at the outlet. New dryer rules it out as the appliance. I'll megger the circuit and check the terminal.
Great, book me whenever you can.
Friday afternoon. Text you a window.
Meggered the dryer circuit Friday, loose connection at the outlet terminal. Re-terminated, no more tripping. Closing.
Do you do panel labeling and a safety inspection? Just bought the place and nothing is labeled.
Do full panel mapping and a visual safety check. Couple hours of work. Want me to flag any code issues while I'm in there?
Need two ceiling fans installed where there's no existing box. Can you fish the wiring?
Fans need a rated fan box and a switch leg. Fishing it depends on attic access above. One story or two?
Both rooms are top floor, attic right above. Let's do it.
Good access then. Half day. Set for Wednesday.
Installed both fan boxes and switch legs Wednesday, fished the wiring from the attic. Spinning quiet, paid on completion. Done.
Want to add a subpanel in the garage for a workshop. 240v tools, a compressor, lighting. Possible?
Subpanel is the right call for that load. Need to size the feeder off your main. What's the main service, 100 or 200?
200 amp main. Garage is detached, about 40 feet from the house.
Detached means a buried feeder and a grounding electrode at the garage. Permit job. Out Monday to measure the trench.
Measured the trench Monday and drew up the buried feeder and ground rod plan for the detached garage. Permit submitted, scheduling the dig. Closing.
Neighbor recommended you. We're in Fox Chase too. Want under cabinet lighting in the kitchen and an island outlet. Doable?
Both doable. Island outlet has to be code compliant, usually in the side of the island. Under cabinet I run hardwired LED. Send a photo of the kitchen.
Do you replace old aluminum wiring? Home inspector in Havertown said our outlets have it.
Aluminum branch wiring I handle with copper pigtails and rated connectors, or a full rewire if it's bad. How many devices did he flag?
He counted about 18 outlets and switches. We just want it safe and to close on the sale.
Pigtailing 18 devices is a one day job. I'll give you a written statement for the buyer. Out Tuesday.
Pigtailed all 18 aluminum devices Tuesday with rated connectors. Gave them the written statement for the buyer. Done.
EV charger question. I have a level 2 charger but it's only getting half speed. Wiring problem?
Half speed usually means the breaker or the charger is set to a lower amp limit than the circuit. Who installed it and what breaker is it on?
Previous owner had it done. 40 amp breaker I think. Charger settings I'm not sure.
Likely the charger amp setting is capped. I'll verify the wire gauge supports the breaker, then set it right. Quick visit. Friday.
Friday visit, the charger amp setting was capped below the circuit. Verified the wire gauge and reset it to full speed. Closing.
We manage a few rental units in Northeast Philly. Need a licensed electrician on call for panel and outlet repairs across the properties. Do you take on accounts like that?
Take on landlord work in Northeast Philly. I'm a one man shop so scheduling has to be realistic, but repairs and violations I cover. How many units?
Six units right now, mostly twins. A couple have older panels we'd want assessed.
Start with the two older panels, I'll write up what each needs. Send addresses and I'll line up the first walkthrough.
We run a small cafe and want to add an espresso machine that needs a dedicated 240v circuit. Also our panel might be maxed. Can you assess?
Commercial espresso needs its own circuit, agreed. If the panel is maxed we look at a subpanel. What's the service size on the building?
Not sure, the landlord handles the main. We lease the space. The machine pulls 30 amps at 240.
You'll need the landlord's sign off for a commercial permit. Get me the panel access and I'll size it. Out Monday to look.
Looked at the cafe Monday. Panel is the landlord's, so waiting on his sign off before pulling the commercial permit. Closing for now, will reopen when he responds.
